fitness-sparse.content:
# The Executive Athlete Sales Call Script

*For Marcus Reed — Reed Performance*

---

## SECTION 1: INTRODUCTION & RAPPORT (0:00-0:01)

"Hey [Name], Marcus here. Good to finally connect with you directly. How's your day treating you so far?"

[DELIVERY NOTE: Keep this warm but brief. Match their energy level.]

"Before we jump in — I saw on your application that you're running [their company type]. That's gotta keep you busy. How long have you been at the helm there?"

[NOTE: 60 seconds max. Don't get stuck in small talk.]

---

## SECTION 2: FRAME SETTING (0:01-0:02)

"Alright, here's how I like to run these calls. I'm going to ask you some questions to understand exactly where you are right now and what you're dealing with. Then, if I think I can genuinely help you, I'll walk you through what that looks like. If I don't think we're a good fit, I'll tell you that too and point you in the right direction."

"This isn't some high-pressure pitch. I work with 12 guys at a time, max. So I'm pretty selective about who I take on. My goal is to figure out if we should work together. Sound fair?"

[Wait for agreement]

"Cool. Let's start with the real stuff."

---

## SECTION 3: PROBLEM ISOLATION (0:02-0:03)

"So tell me — what's the biggest challenge you're facing with your health and fitness right now? What made you reach out?"

[CRITICAL NOTE: Shut up and listen. Do not interrupt. Do not solve yet. Let them talk for 2-3 minutes if they need to.]

"And how long has this been going on?"

---

## SECTION 4: PROBLEM UNDERSTANDING — DEEP PROBING (0:03-0:11)

**Chunking Questions:**

"When you say [repeat their exact words], what does that actually look like day-to-day? Walk me through a typical week."

"Can you give me a specific example from this past week where this showed up?"

"What's your current routine look like? When do you try to work out, if at all?"

"How many hours are you putting in at the office right now? Including the stuff you do from home after dinner?"

**Consequence Questions:**

"How is this affecting your energy levels during the day? When do you typically hit that wall?"

"What about at home — how's this impacting your time with your family?"

"If nothing changes in the next [COACH: Insert your real timeframe here], where does that leave you? What does that look like?"

"You mentioned you're [age] — your dad, how was his health around this age?"

**Feeling Questions:**

"How does it feel when you catch yourself in the mirror getting dressed in the morning?"

"What's the worst part about this situation for you personally?"

"When was the last time you felt really strong? Really confident about how you looked?"

"What happens when you're at company events or client dinners? How do you feel in those situations?"

[NOTE: Spend 8-10 minutes here. This is where you uncover the real pain. Reference their specific triggers: embarrassment in mirrors, exhaustion by 3pm, dreading physicals.]

---

## SECTION 5: DOUBT CREATION (0:11-0:12)

"So you've been trying to handle this on your own... how's that been working out for you?"

[NOTE: They'll usually laugh or say "obviously not great."]

"Right. So what do you think has been missing? Why hasn't it stuck?"

"You're clearly smart enough to run a successful company. So why is this piece so damn hard to figure out?"

---

## SECTION 6: PAST SOLUTION ANALYSIS (0:12-0:15)

"What else have you tried to solve this? Any trainers, programs, apps, whatever?"

"Let me guess — you've got a Peloton collecting dust somewhere?"

[NOTE: Most will laugh and say yes]

"What happened with that? And any other approaches you've tried?"

"What worked initially? What made you stop?"

"Looking back, what do you think was missing from those approaches?"

[NOTE: Listen for patterns — generic programs, no accountability, doesn't fit their schedule, trainer didn't understand their life.]

---

## SECTION 7: SUPPORT ASSESSMENT (0:15-0:17)

"Who's helping you with this right now? Any trainer, nutritionist, anybody?"

"So you're basically figuring this out solo while running a company and managing everything else?"

"How are you making decisions about your health — just winging it based on whatever you read online?"

[NOTE: Most successful executives are used to having support systems for everything except their health.]

---

## SECTION 8: DESIRE EXPLORATION (0:17-0:17)

"Alright, let's flip this. If we could wave a magic wand and fix this — what would your health and fitness look like 6 months from now?"

"Walk me through your ideal day. You wake up, you look in the mirror — what do you see? How do you feel?"

"What would it mean to you to be the guy who's in better shape at 45 than he was at 35?"

"How would that change how you show up as a leader? As a dad?"

[NOTE: Let them paint their own picture. Reference "executive athlete" language — someone who takes their body as seriously as their P&L.]

---

## SECTION 9: WHY NOW / COST OF INACTION (0:17-0:21)

"Let me ask you something serious. You've been dealing with this for [timeframe they mentioned]. What has that already cost you?"

"I'm not just talking money. I'm talking energy, confidence, time with your kids, showing up as the leader you want to be."

"And if nothing changes — if you're sitting here having this exact same conversation [COACH: Insert your real timeframe here] from now — what does that look like?"

"Your dad — how old was he when his health started going downhill?"

"Is that acceptable to you? Because that's the trajectory you're on right now."

[NOTE: Reference their specific fears — dying young like their father, missing their daughter's wedding, heart attack before 55.]

---

## SECTION 10: CLARITY TIE-DOWN (0:21-0:23)

"Let me make sure I understand where you are. You're running a successful company, putting in 60-hour weeks, and your health has taken a backseat for the past [timeframe]. You've tried [list what they mentioned] but nothing has stuck because [their reasons]. You want to [their desired outcome] but you need a system that actually fits the reality of your schedule and travel. Does that sound right?"

"Did I miss anything important?"

[NOTE: If they say "yes, exactly" — you've built trust. If they correct you, adjust and re-confirm.]

---

## SECTION 11: DISCOVERY COMMITMENT (0:23-0:24)

"Based on everything you've shared, I think I can help you. Would you like to hear how I'd approach this?"

[NOTE: They'll almost always say yes. If they hesitate, that's fine — enthusiasm comes during the pitch.]

---

## SECTION 12: TRANSITION TO PITCH (0:24-0:26)

"Here's what I've noticed from everything you've shared. The reason the Peloton and the local trainers haven't worked isn't because you lack willpower or motivation. It's because they're built for people who have predictable schedules and can work out at the same time every day."

"You don't need another generic program. You need a system that syncs to the rhythm of a 60-hour work week. That accounts for business travel, client dinners, and the fact that some weeks you're home, some weeks you're in three different cities."

"What I've built is called The Performance Stack. It's a 3-layer system that treats your body like the high-performance asset it needs to be. Here's how it works..."

---

## SECTION 13: THE PITCH — 3-PHASE SOLUTION (0:26-0:35)

**"Phase 1: Assessment"**

"First [COACH: Insert your real timeframe here], we do a complete assessment. Three video calls plus a full bloodwork review. I need to know exactly where you're starting from — your current fitness level, your bloodwork, your travel schedule, your home setup, everything."

"Remember when you said you're exhausted by 3pm? A lot of that is metabolic. We're going to find out exactly what's going on under the hood. This gives us a custom baseline so we're not guessing."

**"Phase 2: Rebuild"**

"Next 8 weeks, we rebuild your foundation. Custom in-home strength program plus a nutrition protocol that works with business travel. No more hoping you'll find time to get to the gym. We're building a system that works in your home office, hotel rooms, wherever you are."

"You mentioned you haven't felt strong in years? This is where that changes. We're targeting that first 15-20 pound loss while building real strength. You'll start seeing changes in the mirror and feeling them in your energy levels."

**"Phase 3: Scale"**

"Final 12 weeks, we scale everything up. Progressive loading, advanced travel protocols, and we build the maintenance system that keeps you lean and strong long-term."

"This is where you go from the guy who avoids the mirror to the executive athlete — someone who takes his body as seriously as his P&L. You'll have a system that travels with you, adapts to your schedule, and actually gets stronger over time."

[NOTE: Throughout the pitch, reference THEIR specific words from discovery. "You mentioned X — Phase 2 directly addresses that."]

---

## SECTION 14: TEMPERATURE CHECK (0:36-0:39)

"So on a scale of 1 to 10, how does this approach feel so far?"

**If 7+:** "What would make it a 10 for you?"

**If 5-6:** "What's holding you back? What doesn't feel right?"

**If below 5:** "Sounds like this might not be the right fit, and that's totally fine. What specifically doesn't resonate?"

[NOTE: The temperature check reveals hidden objections. Don't skip this step.]

---

## SECTION 15: TRANSITION TO CLOSE (0:39-0:40)

"Great. So would you like to hear what it looks like to actually get started?"

[NOTE: This is asking permission to present the offer details — NOT the close itself.]

---

## SECTION 16: DELIVERABLES & SUPPORT (0:40-0:41)

"Here's exactly what you get: Custom training program built for your home setup, nutrition protocol that works with your travel schedule, weekly video check-ins where we review your progress and adjust everything, biweekly 1-on-1 calls with me, plus private Slack access so you're never stuck wondering what to do."

"The program runs for 6 months. Everything is delivered through a private portal you can access from anywhere. And I personally review every single check-in video you send."

"You'll never be stuck wondering what to do next. Any questions about how it works?"

---

## SECTION 17: INVESTMENT REVEAL (0:41-0:42)

"The investment for The Executive Athlete Program is $12,000 for 6 months."

[CRITICAL NOTE: State the price and STOP TALKING. Let them process. Do not fill the silence.]

"To put that in perspective — you mentioned you're probably wasting $500 a month on unused gym memberships and canceled trainers. This pays for itself when you stop throwing money at solutions that don't work."

"We also offer a payment plan if that works better for your cash flow."

---

## SECTION 18: OBJECTION HANDLING

**Objection: "I need to think about it"**

"I totally get that. Can I ask — what specifically do you need to think about?"

[Wait for their answer]

"In my experience, 'I need to think about it' usually means there's a specific concern I haven't addressed yet. What is it?"

**Objection: "I can't afford it"**

"I hear you. Let me ask — is it that the money literally doesn't exist, or that you're not sure if it's worth the investment?"

**If literal:** "Would the payment plan make this possible for you?"

**If value:** "What would it be worth to you to go from where you are now to feeling strong and confident again? What's it worth to not end up like your dad at 58?"

**Objection: "I've tried coaching before"**

"What specifically didn't work with the other approaches?"

[Listen to their answer]

"That makes sense. The difference with The Performance Stack is [address their specific gap]. What was missing before was exactly what we focus on — a system that actually fits your life instead of forcing you to fit into someone else's program."

**Objection: "I don't have time to train"**

"That's exactly why this works. You're not going to the gym for 90 minutes. We're talking 15-20 minute sessions you can do in your home office. The guy who's too busy for 15 minutes is the guy who needs this most."

**Objection: "Online coaching can't replace in-person"**

"You're right — if we were talking about learning to deadlift 500 pounds. But we're talking about getting you lean, strong, and energetic while working around a 60-hour week. I can see your form on video, adjust your program in real-time, and I'm available when you actually need help — not just during gym hours."

**Objection: "I've tried this before and it didn't stick"**

"What made you stop the last time?"

[Listen]

"Right. So the issue wasn't the workouts themselves — it was that the system didn't account for your real life. Business travel, long days, client dinners. That's exactly what The Performance Stack is built for."

**Objection: "I need to talk to my wife"**

"Absolutely — you should be aligned on this. What specifically do you think she'd want to know?"

"Would it help if I sent her the program details, or would you prefer to walk through it with her first?"

---

## SECTION 19: THE CLOSE (0:42+)

**If ready:**
"Awesome. Let me get you set up right now. I'll send you the enrollment link and we'll schedule your first assessment call for this week."

**If needs time:**
"No pressure at all. I'll send you a summary of everything we discussed today. When's a good time to follow up — tomorrow or Thursday?"

**If no:**
"I respect that decision. If anything changes, you know where to find me. In the meantime, here's what I'd focus on: stop trying to fit generic programs into your schedule. Find something built for your life, not someone else's."

[NOTE: Never beg, negotiate on price, or create fake urgency. Stay professional.]

---

## SECTION 20: POST-CALL SUMMARY

**Template to fill out after each call:**

- **Prospect name:**
- **Company/Industry:**
- **Key problem identified:**
- **Current situation (hours/week, travel, family):**
- **What they've tried before:**
- **What resonated most:**
- **Objections raised:**
- **Temperature check score:**
- **Outcome (closed / follow-up / not a fit):**
- **Follow-up date:**
- **Notes for next conversation:**

---

### GUARANTEE CLOSE (Use if needed)

"One more thing — I'm so confident this will work for you that if you don't lose 20 lbs in the first 90 days while following the protocol, I'll work with you for free until you do. I'm putting my reputation on the line here because I know this system works."

---

### SCRIPT PRINCIPLES REMINDER

- **Discovery takes 50% of the call** — the more pain you uncover, the easier the close
- **Reference their exact words** throughout the pitch — "You mentioned earlier..."
- **Stay conversational** — this is a dialogue, not a monologue
- **Never be pushy** — "I don't chase. You jump, I jump."
- **Silence is your friend** — especially after stating the price
- **Address objections directly** — don't dance around concerns

**Total call time: 45-60 minutes**

# Reed Performance Homepage Copy

## Hero Section

**Headline:** The Executive Athlete: Build the Body That Matches Your Business Success

**Subheadline:** Stop letting your health be the one area where you're failing. If you're a high-performing executive who's tried 3+ fitness approaches without lasting results, it's time for a system that actually fits your 60-hour work week.

**Primary CTA:** Apply for The Executive Athlete Program

**Hero Image Suggestion:** Professional executive in a suit jacket and athletic wear, working out in a modern home office with a laptop visible in the background.

---

## The Problem

Here's what I know about you: You built a company that generates $300K+ per year. You make decisions that affect dozens of people. You can negotiate million-dollar deals and manage complex operations.

But when you catch your reflection in the elevator mirror each morning, you see someone you don't recognize. You're exhausted by 3pm, dreading your next physical, and avoiding photos at company events. You're embarrassed when you catch your reflection, and you know you're one stress test away from the heart attack your father had at 58.

You've tried the Peloton bike gathering dust in your basement. You've hired local trainers you cancel on because of last-minute client calls. You've downloaded MyFitnessPal and abandoned it after 2 weeks when a business trip derailed everything.

The problem isn't your work ethic. The problem is every fitness program treats you like you have the schedule of a college student, not the reality of running a company.

Sound familiar? You're not broken. You just need a system built for executives, not gym bros.

---

## The Performance Stack

The Executive Athlete Program isn't another cookie-cutter fitness plan. It's The Performance Stack — a 3-layer system that syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to the rhythm of a 60-hour work week.

**Phase 1: Assessment (3 video calls + bloodwork review)**
We establish your custom baseline. No guessing, no generic programs. We map your current health markers, schedule constraints, and travel patterns to build a system that works with your life, not against it.

**Phase 2: Rebuild (8 weeks of in-home strength + nutrition protocol)**
You'll lose your first 15-20 lbs while building a strength baseline using equipment that fits in your home office. We teach you nutrition protocols that work during business dinners and client meetings.

**Phase 3: Scale (12 weeks of progressive loading + travel protocols)**
Complete transformation with maintenance systems. You'll know exactly how to train through jet lag, use hotel gyms effectively, and maintain your results while traveling 2 weeks per month.

This isn't about finding more time. It's about using the time you have strategically.

---

## Results Section

**The Transformation:** From burnt-out executive who avoids the mirror to a strong, lean, high-energy leader who trains on his own terms.

**What You Get:** Lose 30 lbs in 6 months without missing a single business meeting. Build strength that carries into your boardroom presence. Develop energy that lasts past 3pm and into evening time with your family.

Dan M., SaaS CEO, was burned out, 42 lbs over target, and hadn't trained in 3 years. After 6 months in The Executive Athlete Program, he lost 42 lbs, squats 2x bodyweight, and travels 2 weeks per month while staying on track. "Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like."

Rick P., private equity partner, came to me prediabetic with an A1C of 6.2. After our 6-month program and nutrition overhaul, his A1C dropped to 5.4 and he lost 28 lbs. "My doctor couldn't believe the numbers."

**My Guarantee:** If you don't lose 20 lbs in the first 90 days while following the protocol, I work with you free until you do.

---

## About Marcus Reed

In 2021, I was training 12 hours a day at my gym and still barely breaking even after lease and payroll. I was burnt out, gaining weight, and resenting clients. I was the fat kid nobody believed in, and I was failing the very people I set out to help.

Then COVID hit. A client asked if I could train him over Zoom. I said yes, and realized I could help just as many people without a physical location. I closed the gym in 2022 and went fully online, focusing exclusively on executives who needed results without the gym commute.

I've coached 340 clients over 11 years, but my real expertise comes from understanding what it's like to run a business while trying to stay healthy. I know what it's like to choose between a client call and a workout. I know what it's like to eat airport food for a week straight.

That's why I created The Executive Athlete Program — because high performers deserve a fitness system as sophisticated as their business strategy.

**Certifications:** NASM CPT, Precision Nutrition L1

---

## This Is NOT For You If...

- You're looking for a quick fix or magic pill
- You expect results without following the protocol
- You're not willing to invest in custom programming
- You want someone to coddle you through excuses
- You think online coaching can't replace your canceled gym sessions

If that's you, please don't apply. I work with executives who take action, not those who collect programs.

---

## This IS For You If...

- You're ready to treat your body like the asset it is
- You understand that systems beat willpower every time
- You're tired of programs that ignore your travel schedule
- You want accountability from someone who gets your lifestyle
- You're committed to becoming the executive athlete you know you can be

You're a high performer in business. It's time to become a high performer in health.

---

## Ready to Become The Executive Athlete?

Stop letting your health be the one area where you're failing. You've built a successful company by implementing the right systems. Now it's time to apply that same approach to your body.

The Executive Athlete Program is limited to 12 clients at a time because each program is fully customized. If you're ready to lose 30 lbs in 6 months without missing a single business meeting, and you meet the criteria above, apply below.

**Apply for The Executive Athlete Program**

Your business success proves you can execute when you have the right system. Let's build that system for your health.

# About Marcus Reed

Most executives can deadlift their quarterly numbers but can't deadlift their bodyweight. You've built a company that runs without you, but your body is falling apart with you in it.

## How I Went From Burnt-Out Gym Owner to the Go-To Coach for High-Performing Executives

In 2021, I was that guy everyone thought "had it made." I owned Reed Performance gym in Austin, had a steady stream of clients, and looked successful from the outside. The reality? I was training 12 hours a day and still barely breaking even after lease and payroll. I was burnt out, gaining weight, and resenting the very clients I was supposed to help.

Sound familiar? I was living the fitness industry version of what most executives experience — working harder than everyone else but somehow getting further behind.

The turning point came during COVID when a client asked if I could train him over Zoom. I said yes, mostly because I needed the money. But something clicked during that first virtual session. I realized I could help just as many people — maybe more — without being chained to a physical location. Without the overhead eating my profits. Without the 12-hour days that were killing my own health.

I closed the gym in 2022 and went fully online. Now I coach executives remotely with customized home programs that actually fit their reality. No more "just find time for the gym" bullshit. No more cookie-cutter programs that ignore business travel and 60-hour weeks.

Here's what I discovered: The executives who succeed in business but fail at fitness aren't lacking willpower. They're lacking a system that respects the demands of running a company. Once I built that system — The Performance Stack — everything changed.

## What I Believe

After 11 years and 340 clients, here's what I know to be true:

• **Generic gym programs are designed for people with generic lives.** If your calendar looks like Tetris and your phone buzzes with "urgent" requests at 9 PM, you need a training system built for that reality, not some fantasy where you have 90 minutes every morning.

• **The real problem isn't time — it's energy management.** Most executives think they need more hours in the day. What they actually need is more energy in the hours they have. That comes from training your body like the high-performance machine it needs to be.

• **You don't need a gym membership to get in the best shape of your life.** Some of my strongest clients haven't set foot in a commercial gym in years. A few pieces of equipment in your home office beats a fancy gym you never visit.

• **Investing in your health is the highest-ROI decision you'll make.** You'll spend $500 a month on software you barely use, but hesitate to invest in the one asset that determines whether you're around to enjoy your success.

• **Travel doesn't have to derail everything.** I've had clients maintain their programs through IPO roadshows, international expansions, and merger negotiations. It's about having protocols, not excuses.

• **The best time to start was 5 years ago — the second best time is right now.** Every month you wait is another month closer to the heart attack your doctor keeps warning you about.

## The Track Record

• **11 years** training executives, entrepreneurs, and high performers
• **340 total clients** coached through transformations
• **NASM CPT** and **Precision Nutrition L1** certified
• **52 out of 60 clients** in my Executive Athlete Program hit their target results

But here's the number that matters most: Zero clients have had to choose between their business success and their physical transformation. The system works because it's built for people who refuse to accept that trade-off.

## Beyond the Business

When I'm not helping executives reclaim their energy, you'll find me running the trails around Austin, reading everything from business biographies to barbecue techniques (I take my brisket as seriously as my training protocols), or trying to keep up with my two kids.

I was raised in Ohio by a single mom who worked two jobs and never complained. She taught me that relentless work ethic isn't just about grinding harder — it's about being smart enough to build systems that work even when you're not perfect.

That's what I bring to every client: the understanding that you're not looking for another thing to manage. You're looking for a system that manages itself while delivering results that compound over time.

## Ready to Stop Accepting "Good Enough"?

If you're tired of catching your reflection in the elevator mirror every morning and making the same promise to "start next week," we should talk.

The Executive Athlete Program isn't for executives who want to dabble in fitness. It's for leaders who are ready to take their body as seriously as their P&L.

**[Apply for The Executive Athlete Program →]**

Because the best investment you'll ever make isn't in your business. It's in the person running it.

# Welcome Email Sequence for Reed Performance

---

## Email 1: Welcome + Delivery

**Subject Line:** Your 15-minute workout is here (+ what's next)
**Alternative:** The Executive's 15-Minute Workout [attached]

**Preview Text:** Download inside + 6 more emails coming your way this week

**Body Copy:**

You downloaded The Executive's 15-Minute Workout for a reason.

Maybe you're tired of catching your reflection in the elevator mirror each morning. Maybe you're exhausted by 3pm and dreading your next physical.

Either way, you're in the right place.

I'm Marcus Reed. I've spent 11 years helping executives get in the best shape of their lives without leaving their home office. 340 clients coached. Most of them just like you — running companies, making tough decisions, and putting their health last.

Your workout guide is attached. It's designed for guys who have 15 minutes between meetings and zero patience for fluff.

Over the next week, I'm sending you 6 more emails. No sales pitches. Just the truth about why every approach you've tried has failed — and what actually works for executives who travel 2 weeks per month and work 60-hour weeks.

Hit reply and tell me: what's the biggest obstacle keeping you from getting in shape right now?

Talk soon,
Marcus

P.S. The workout in that guide? My client Dan (SaaS CEO) used it during his first month. Six months later, he lost 42 lbs and squats 2x his bodyweight. Still travels constantly. Still stays on track.

**CTA:** Hit reply with your biggest obstacle

---

## Email 2: Origin Story → Hell Island to Heaven Island
**Send:** Day 1-2  
**Purpose:** Build trust through shared struggle, show transformation is possible

**Subject Line:** How I went from burnt-out gym owner to online coach
**Alternative:** The day I realized everything was broken

**Preview Text:** My 2021 wake-up call (and why it matters to you)

**Body Copy:**

In 2021, I was training 12 hours a day at my gym in Austin.

Still barely breaking even after lease and payroll. Burnt out. Gaining weight. Resenting my clients.

Sound familiar?

I was the guy who was supposed to have it figured out. Personal trainer for [COACH: Insert your real timeframe here]. Owned Reed Performance gym since 2015. Helped hundreds of people get in shape.

But I was embarrassed when I caught my own reflection. Exhausted by 3pm. Dreading my own workouts.

The irony wasn't lost on me.

Then COVID hit. A client asked if I could train him over Zoom. I said yes, mostly because I needed the money.

That single session changed everything.

I realized I could help just as many people without a physical location. Without the overhead. Without the 12-hour days that were killing me.

I closed the gym in 2022 and went fully online.

Now I coach executives remotely with customized home programs. I work with 12 clients at a time instead of 40. I'm in the best shape of my life. And my clients get better results than they ever did in person.

The transformation wasn't just physical. It was mental.

I went from resenting my work to loving it again. From being exhausted to being energized. From avoiding mirrors to being proud of what I saw.

Here's what I learned: the problem was never the people I was trying to help.

The problem was the system.

Tomorrow I'll tell you why every approach you've tried has failed — and it's not what you think.

Marcus

**CTA:** No specific action — engagement hook for next email

---

## Email 3: Core Belief #1 — Your Current Method Is the Problem
**Send:** Day 3-4  
**Purpose:** Challenge false belief about current approach, address time objection

**Subject Line:** Why you don't have time to train (it's not what you think)
**Alternative:** The real reason your Peloton is gathering dust

**Preview Text:** Hint: It's not your schedule that's the problem

**Body Copy:**

"I don't have time to train."

I hear this from every executive who applies to work with me.

Here's the thing: you're right. You don't have time.

You don't have time for 90-minute gym sessions. You don't have time to meal prep on Sundays. You don't have time for programs designed for 25-year-olds who live at the gym.

But that's not a YOU problem. That's a METHOD problem.

Every trainer you've hired gave you the same cookie-cutter approach:
- Train 5-[COACH: Insert your real timeframe here] per week
- Spend an hour each session
- Follow a rigid meal plan
- Track everything in MyFitnessPal

Then they act surprised when you cancel sessions for business travel. Or when you abandon the program after 2 weeks.

The method is broken. Not you.

You need a system that works WITH your 60-hour weeks, not against them.

You need protocols for hotel gyms. Nutrition strategies for client dinners. Workouts that fit between meetings.

You need training that syncs to the rhythm of running a company, not the schedule of someone who has all day to optimize their macros.

The executives I work with don't have more time than you do. Dan travels 2 weeks per month. Rick works 70-hour weeks during deal season.

They just use a different method.

A method designed for people who take their P&L as seriously as their health.

Tomorrow I'll show you what that method looks like.

Marcus

**CTA:** No specific action — sets up next email

---

## Email 4: Core Belief #2 — The Real Problem Is Not What You Think
**Send:** Day 5-6  
**Purpose:** Redefine the real problem, introduce unique mechanism

**Subject Line:** You're solving the wrong problem (here's the real one)
**Alternative:** Why willpower isn't your issue

**Preview Text:** The hidden reason online coaching actually works better

**Body Copy:**

You think your problem is willpower.

You think you just need to "want it more" or "be more disciplined."

Wrong.

Your problem is that you're trying to force a consumer fitness approach into an executive lifestyle.

Consumer fitness assumes:
- You have the same schedule every day
- You can control your meal timing
- You can train at the same gym consistently
- You have 60-90 minutes to dedicate to workouts

Executive reality:
- Your schedule changes hourly
- You eat when deals allow it
- You're in a different city every week
- You have 15-20 minutes between calls

See the disconnect?

The real problem isn't your willpower. It's that nobody has built a system that fits your actual life.

That's why I created The Performance Stack.

It's a 3-layer system that syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to the rhythm of a 60-hour work week.

Layer 1: Micro-workouts that scale with your schedule (15-45 minutes)
Layer 2: Business-travel nutrition protocols (no meal prep required)
Layer 3: Recovery strategies that work in hotel rooms

The entire system is designed around one principle: your fitness has to adapt to your business, not the other way around.

This is why online coaching actually works BETTER for executives than in-person training.

In-person training locks you into someone else's schedule. Online coaching adapts to yours.

You get protocols for every scenario. Hotel gym workouts. Airport meal strategies. How to train through jet lag.

You get a system that travels with you instead of holding you back.

Tomorrow I'll show you proof this actually works.

Marcus

**CTA:** No specific action — builds toward case studies

---

## Email 5: Core Belief #5 — Why This Approach Works
**Send:** Day 7-8  
**Purpose:** Provide proof through case studies, address "tried before" objection

**Subject Line:** How Dan lost 42 lbs while traveling 2 weeks per month
**Alternative:** Two executives, same system, different results

**Preview Text:** Real results from guys just like you

**Body Copy:**

Let me tell you about Dan.

SaaS CEO. Burned out. 42 lbs over his target weight. Hadn't trained in 3 years.

Sound familiar?

Dan's problem wasn't motivation. He'd tried everything:
- Hired 3 different personal trainers (canceled on all of them)
- Bought a Peloton (used it twice)
- Downloaded MyFitnessPal (abandoned after 2 weeks)

His problem was that every approach required him to change his business to fit his fitness.

When Dan started The Executive Athlete Program, we flipped the script.

Instead of forcing him into a rigid schedule, we built his training around his reality. Micro-workouts between meetings. Travel protocols for his monthly trips to New York. Nutrition strategies that worked during client dinners.

Six months later: Lost 42 lbs. Squats 2x his bodyweight. Still travels 2 weeks per month. Still stays on track.

"Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like."

Then there's Rick.

Private equity partner. Prediabetic with an A1C of 6.2. His doctor was pushing medication.

Rick didn't need another lecture about eating better. He needed a system that worked during 16-hour deal days.

We overhauled his nutrition using the same travel protocols. Added strength training that fit his schedule.

Six months later: A1C dropped to 5.4. Lost 28 lbs. Doctor couldn't believe the numbers.

"My doctor couldn't believe the numbers."

Same system. Different guys. Both got results they couldn't get anywhere else.

The difference? We didn't try to change their lives to fit a program. We built a program that fit their lives.

That's The Performance Stack in action.

Tomorrow I'll show you what happens if you keep doing what you're doing now.

Marcus

**CTA:** No specific action — sets up urgency email

---

## Email 6: Core Belief #4 — The Cost of Inaction + Investment Reframe
**Send:** Day 9-10  
**Purpose:** Create urgency through cost of inaction, reframe investment

**Subject Line:** The cost of waiting until "next quarter"
**Alternative:** What happens if you do nothing

**Preview Text:** Every month of delay has a price tag

**Body Copy:**

"I'll start after this quarter ends."

I hear this excuse constantly.

Here's what happens while you wait:

- **Productivity gains from increased energy:** [COACH: Insert your realistic estimate of additional revenue from improved performance]

**Value Reframe Statement:**

> "The real question isn't whether you can afford $12,000 for 6 months. It's whether you can afford every month of delay = another month closer to the heart attack his father had at 58, plus ~$500/month wasted on unused Peloton memberships and canceled trainers. At conservative estimates, this program pays for itself through eliminated waste alone, and the productivity gains from having your energy back generate [COACH: Insert 12-month ROI estimate] over the next [COACH: Insert your real timeframe here]."

---

## SECTION 3: GUARANTEE LANGUAGE

**Coach's Current Guarantee Analysis:**
Your existing guarantee ("If you don't lose 20 lbs in the first 90 days while following the protocol, I work with you free until you do") is strong because it's specific, measurable, and shows confidence. Recommend keeping it but adding a compliance clause for clarity.

**Version 1: THE BOLD GUARANTEE (Results-Based)**
> "If you complete all weekly check-ins, follow the nutrition protocol, and execute the training program for 90 days and don't lose at least 20 lbs, I'll continue working with you at no additional cost until you hit that milestone. Period."

*Use this when you have strong case studies and confidence in your system.*

**Version 2: THE BALANCED GUARANTEE (Implementation-Based)**
> "Complete all 12 weekly video check-ins, submit your progress photos, and follow the program for 90 days. If you've done the work and aren't completely satisfied with your progress, I'll refund your entire investment."

*Use this for prospects who seem committed but want reassurance about the process.*

**Version 3: THE CONSERVATIVE GUARANTEE (Satisfaction-Based)**
> "Try The Executive Athlete Program for [COACH: Insert your real timeframe here]. If it's not the right fit for your lifestyle, I'll refund your investment. No questions asked."

*Use this when testing new markets or if you're not confident in your current system.*

**Recommendation:** Strengthen your current guarantee by adding the compliance requirements explicitly. This protects you from prospects who don't follow through while maintaining the bold promise.

---

## SECTION 4: SCARCITY & URGENCY OPTIONS

**3 Legitimate Scarcity Tactics**

1. **Limited Coaching Capacity**
   - **Why it's legitimate:** You can only handle 12 active clients at a time for quality delivery
   - **Language:** "I work with a maximum of 12 executives at any time to ensure you get the attention your transformation deserves. Currently, I have [X] spots available for new clients starting [month]."

2. **Quarterly Enrollment Windows**
   - **Why it's legitimate:** Allows you to batch onboarding and maintain program quality
   - **Language:** "The Executive Athlete Program opens for new enrollment four times per year. The next cohort begins [date], with enrollment closing [date]."

3. **Bonus Access Limitations**
   - **Why it's legitimate:** Private Slack access becomes unwieldy with too many members
   - **Language:** "Private Slack access is limited to current program participants only. Once this cohort fills, new clients will join the next available group."

**3 Legitimate Urgency Tactics**

1. **Cost of Delay (Health-Based)**
   - **Real reason:** Health risks compound with age and delay
   - **Language:** "Every month of delay = another month closer to the heart attack his father had at 58. Plus ~$500/month wasted on unused Peloton memberships and canceled trainers. The question isn't whether to start — it's whether to start now or keep paying the price of waiting."

2. **Price Increase Schedule**
   - **Real reason:** You're raising prices as demand increases
   - **Language:** "The Executive Athlete Program is $12,000 for 6 months through [date]. Starting [next date], the investment increases to [new price] to reflect increased demand and program enhancements."

3. **Seasonal Timing**
   - **Real reason:** Executive schedules and motivation cycles
   - **Language:** "Starting in January means you'll be in peak shape for summer executive retreats and family vacations. Waiting until spring means missing the optimal transformation window."

**Recommended Fast-Action Bonus**
- **Bonus:** Quarterly bloodwork review and analysis (normally $500 value)
- **Presentation:** "Enroll by [72 hours from now] and get a comprehensive quarterly bloodwork review and analysis — a $500 addition, yours free. This ensures we're tracking your internal health markers alongside the external transformation."
- **Why it's valuable:** Executives appreciate data-driven health monitoring and this bonus directly supports the program's health outcomes.

# Facebook Content Library: Marcus Reed

## Post 1: Value Post
**Type:** Value Post  
**Funnel Stage:** TOF  
**Retargeting:** Y - Executives who engaged with fitness content

Most executives think they need 90 minutes a day to get in shape.

Here's what actually works:

15 minutes of compound movements beats 2 hours of isolation exercises.

Why? Because you're not a bodybuilder. You're a business owner who needs functional strength that translates to real life.

The squat teaches you to get up from chairs without groaning.
The deadlift teaches you to pick up your kids without throwing out your back.
The press teaches you to put luggage in overhead bins without looking weak.

Try this today: 5 squats, 5 pushups, 5 burpees. Time it. That's your baseline.

What's the longest you've gone without working out because you "didn't have time"?

---

## Post 2: Story Post
**Type:** Story Post  
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** Y - Warm audience

In 2021, I was training clients 12 hours a day at my gym.

Still barely breaking even after lease and payroll.

I was burnt out, gaining weight, and resenting the very people I was supposed to help.

The irony wasn't lost on me. I was a fitness coach who was embarrassed by his own reflection.

Then COVID hit.

A client asked if I could train him over Zoom. I said yes, figuring it was temporary.

That single call changed everything.

I realized I could help just as many people without the overhead, the commute, the equipment maintenance.

I closed the gym in 2022 and went fully online.

Now I coach executives remotely with customized home programs.

Sometimes the best business decision feels like the scariest one.

What "temporary" solution in your business turned into your biggest breakthrough?

---

## Post 3: Belief-Shifting Post
**Type:** Belief-Shifting Post  
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** Y - Executives who engaged with fitness/health content

Most executives think they just need more willpower to get in shape.

That's like thinking you need more willpower to run a Fortune 500 company with a startup's infrastructure.

You can have all the discipline in the world, but if your systems can't scale with your demands, you'll burn out every time.

Your trainer gives you a program that requires 90 minutes a day and perfect meal timing.

Then your biggest client has an emergency.
Then you have to fly to close a deal.
Then you're eating dinner at 9pm after your kids are in bed.

The program breaks. You blame your willpower.

But willpower is finite. Systems run automatically.

You don't need more discipline. You need infrastructure that matches your demands.

What systems in your business run automatically, even when you're not there?

---

**Type:** Results/Proof Post  
**Funnel Stage:** BOF  
**Retargeting:** Y - Warm audience + fitness interests

Dan was a SaaS CEO.

Burned out. 42 pounds over his target weight. Hadn't trained in 3 years.

He'd tried Peloton. Hired local trainers he kept canceling on. Downloaded MyFitnessPal and abandoned it after 2 weeks.

Sound familiar?

Dan's problem wasn't motivation. It was infrastructure.

He needed a system that worked with his travel schedule, not against it.

6 months later: Lost 42 pounds. Squats 2x his bodyweight. Travels 2 weeks a month and stays on track.

"Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like."

The difference? A program designed for executives who can't fit conventional fitness into their schedule.

Not cookie-cutter. Not one-size-fits-all.

Custom infrastructure for a custom life.

What's the biggest obstacle between you and the shape you want to be in?

**Type:** Value Post  
**Funnel Stage:** TOF  
**Retargeting:** N

Hotel gyms are terrible.

But you can still get a killer workout.

Here's your travel protocol:

**If they have dumbbells:** Goblet squats, single-arm rows, chest press, overhead press. 3 rounds, 10 reps each.

**If they only have cardio machines:** 20 minutes alternating between treadmill sprints and bodyweight exercises in the corner.

**If the gym is closed:** Pushup variations, air squats, lunges, planks in your hotel room. Set a 15-minute timer.

The key isn't perfect equipment. It's consistent movement.

Your business doesn't stop when you travel. Neither should your fitness.

Try this: Take a photo of the next hotel gym you use. Most executives are shocked by what they can accomplish with limited equipment.

What's your biggest challenge with staying consistent while traveling?

---

**Type:** Identity Post  
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** Y - Executives interested in health/fitness

There are two types of successful executives:

The burnt-out business builder who sacrificed his body to build his empire.

And the executive athlete who takes his body as seriously as his P&L.

The burnt-out business builder catches his reflection in the elevator mirror each morning and looks away.

He's exhausted by 3pm.

He dreads his next physical.

He skips his son's baseball games because he's too tired.

The executive athlete takes off his shirt at the beach without sucking in.

He's confident, capable, energetic.

He's proud of how he looks and feels.

He shows up fully present for his family because he has the energy to match his ambition.

Both built successful businesses.

Only one built a life he's proud to live.

Which one are you becoming?

Your health is the foundation everything else is built on.

**Funnel Stage:** TOF

Nutrition while traveling doesn't have to derail your progress.

Here's my executive travel protocol:

**Airport:** Skip the food court. Pack protein bars and nuts. Stay hydrated.

**Business dinners:** Order first. Get the protein and vegetables. Skip the bread basket.

**Hotel breakfast:** Eggs, fruit, coffee. Avoid the pastry spread.

**Late meetings:** Keep emergency protein in your laptop bag. Don't let hunger make decisions for you.

The goal isn't perfection. It's consistency.

One bad meal doesn't ruin your progress. Seven bad meals in a row will.

Most executives think they need perfect conditions to eat well.

Actually, you need systems that work in imperfect conditions.

Try this: Plan your next business trip's meals before you leave. See how much easier it becomes.

# YouTube Video Script: "How Busy CEOs Are Getting in the Best Shape of Their Lives Without Leaving Their Home Office"

**Video Title (SEO):** How Busy CEOs Are Getting in the Best Shape of Their Lives Without Leaving Their Home Office | The Performance Stack Method

**Thumbnail Text:** "Lost 42 lbs | Home Office Only"

**Core Belief Shift:** From "I need more willpower and time to get in shape" to "I need a system that syncs to my 60-hour work week"

**Description:** Male executives making $300K+ are losing 30+ pounds in 6 months without stepping foot in a gym. Marcus Reed reveals The Performance Stack — a 3-layer system that syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to the rhythm of a 60-hour work week. If you're embarrassed when you catch your reflection, exhausted by 3pm, and have tried Peloton, trainers, and apps without lasting results, this changes everything.

---

## 1. HOOK (0:00-0:30) [~75 words]

Listen, if you're a male executive between 40-55 who runs your own company, makes $300K+, and you're embarrassed when you catch your reflection in that elevator mirror each morning... pay attention.

Because I'm going to show you how busy CEOs are losing 30 pounds in 6 months without stepping foot in a gym or missing a single business meeting.

[ON SCREEN: "30 lbs in 6 months | No gym required"]

And if you think you just need more willpower or time to get in shape, you're about to discover why that belief is keeping you stuck.

## 2. INTRO (0:30-1:30) [~150 words]

My name is Marcus Reed. In the last 11 years, I've coached 340 clients and built a system that helps executives get in the best shape of their lives without leaving their home office.

Here's what you're going to learn today:

First, why generic fitness programs fail executives who work 60-hour weeks and travel constantly.

Second, the real reason you're stuck — and it's not what you think.

And third, The Performance Stack — a 3-layer system that syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to your business schedule.

[ON SCREEN: "Marcus Reed | 11 years | 340 clients coached"]

Now, this video is for executives who've tried Peloton bikes, hired trainers they cancel on, and downloaded MyFitnessPal only to abandon it after 2 weeks. If you're looking for some magic pill or 30-day transformation, this isn't for you.

But if you're ready to understand why everything you've tried has failed, keep watching.

## 3. PROBLEM (1:30-4:00) [~375 words]

You probably think you just need more willpower and discipline, right? That if you could just stick to a program for once, you'd finally get results.

Here's the problem with that belief...

[DIRECTION: Lean forward, more intense]

You're trying to run a Fortune 500 company with a startup's infrastructure. You can have all the willpower in the world, but if your systems can't handle your demands, you'll burn out every time.

Think about it. When was the last time you had a predictable week? When was the last time you ate lunch at the same time three days in a row? When was the last time you weren't dealing with a client emergency, business travel, or back-to-back meetings until 8pm?

[ON SCREEN: "When did you last have a predictable week?"]

But here's what happens when you believe it's about willpower...

You download MyFitnessPal and track for two weeks until that acquisition deal demands your full attention. You buy a Peloton and use it twice before it becomes an expensive clothes rack. You hire a trainer who gives you a 90-minute program that assumes you have the schedule of a college student.

And every time you "fail," you tell yourself you just need more discipline. So you try again. Same approach, same result.

Meanwhile, you're catching your reflection in the elevator mirror each morning and feeling embarrassed. You're exhausted by 3pm when you used to power through 12-hour days. You're dreading your next physical because you know what the scale's going to say.

[DIRECTION: Pause for emphasis]

You're skipping your son's baseball games because you're too tired. You're avoiding photos at company events. You're making excuses about why you can't take off your shirt at the beach.

And the worst part? You know you're one heart attack away from becoming another statistic. Just like your father at 58.

[ON SCREEN: "Every month of delay = another month closer"]

This is what happens when you believe fitness is about willpower instead of systems.

## 4. NEW BELIEF / PARADIGM SHIFT (4:00-8:00) [~600 words]

Here's what I discovered that changed everything...

You don't have a willpower problem. You have a system problem.

[DIRECTION: Slow down, let this sink in]

You need infrastructure that matches your demands.

[PROOF BLOCK: Universal Analogy]
It's like trying to use a bicycle to compete in Formula 1. The bicycle isn't broken — it's just the wrong tool for the job. Generic fitness programs are built for people with predictable schedules, not executives who travel 2 weeks a month and work through lunch.

[PROOF BLOCK: Logic Trap]
Think about this logically. If willpower was the solution, then the most successful, disciplined people in the world — executives like you — would have the least trouble getting in shape. But what do we see? The opposite. The higher you climb in business, the worse your health gets. That tells you willpower isn't the variable that matters.

[PROOF BLOCK: Cross-Domain Reference]
In business, you don't try to scale a company with willpower. You build systems. You create processes that run whether you're there or not. You design infrastructure that gets stronger as demands increase. Why would fitness be any different?

[ON SCREEN: "Systems scale. Willpower doesn't."]

[PROOF BLOCK: Personal Story]
I learned this the hard way. In 2021 I was training 12 hours a day at my gym and still barely breaking even after lease and payroll. I was burnt out, gaining weight, and resenting clients. I was the guy helping others get in shape while I was falling apart.

Then COVID hit. A client asked if I could train him over Zoom. I said yes, and realized I could help just as many people without a physical location. So I closed the gym in 2022 and went fully online.

[DIRECTION: More energy, excited]

That's when I discovered something powerful. When I stopped trying to force executives into generic programs and started building systems around their reality, everything changed.

[PROOF BLOCK: Client Story]
Like Dan M., a SaaS CEO who was burned out, 42 pounds over his target weight, and hadn't trained in 3 years. He'd tried everything — Peloton, local trainers, apps. Nothing stuck because nothing fit his reality.

But when we built him a system that synced to his travel schedule and 60-hour weeks, he lost 42 pounds in 6 months. Now he squats 2x his bodyweight and travels 2 weeks a month without missing a workout. As Dan put it: "Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like."

[ON SCREEN: "Dan M. | Lost 42 lbs | Travels 2 weeks/month"]

[PROOF BLOCK: Reframe]
Here's the reframe that changes everything: What if the problem isn't your lack of discipline? What if the problem is that you're trying to use consumer fitness solutions for an executive lifestyle?

[PROOF BLOCK: Authority Reference]
This aligns with what we know from systems thinking. Peter Senge talks about this in "The Fifth Discipline" — when you have a systemic problem, individual effort can't solve it. You need to change the system.

[DIRECTION: Lean in, more direct]

Look, you wouldn't try to run your business with a system designed for a startup. You wouldn't use small business accounting software for a multi-million dollar operation. So why are you trying to get in shape with a program designed for someone with a 9-to-5 job?

The executives who get in the best shape of their lives understand this. They don't try harder — they build better systems.

[ON SCREEN: "Don't try harder. Build better systems."]

That's The Performance Stack — a 3-layer system that syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to the rhythm of a 60-hour work week.

## 5. PROOF / STORIES (8:00-12:00) [~600 words]

Let me tell you exactly how I discovered this...

[DIRECTION: Storytelling mode, more personal]

Back in 2021, I was the poster child for everything wrong with the fitness industry. I owned a gym in Austin, was training clients 12 hours a day, and I was the most out-of-shape person in the building.

I was caught in this trap where I thought if I just worked harder, pushed through the burnout, and showed more discipline, everything would work out. Sound familiar?

But I was gaining weight while helping others lose it. I was exhausted while trying to give others energy. I was resenting the very people I was supposed to be helping.

[DIRECTION: Pause, vulnerability]

I remember catching my reflection in the gym mirror one day and thinking, "How can I help anyone when I can't even help myself?"

Then COVID hit. Everything shut down. I thought my business was over.

But then this client calls me and asks if I can train him over Zoom. I figured, why not? What did I have to lose?

[DIRECTION: Energy building]

That one call changed everything. Not just because it saved my business, but because it forced me to think differently about fitness.

See, when you can't rely on equipment or a gym environment, you have to focus on what actually matters. You have to build systems that work anywhere, anytime, with minimal resources.

I realized I'd been making fitness way more complicated than it needed to be. I'd been trying to force people into my system instead of building a system around their reality.

[ON SCREEN: "Build systems around reality, not ideal conditions"]

So I closed the gym in 2022 and went fully online. Started working exclusively with executives who couldn't fit conventional fitness into their lives.

And that's when I met Rick P., a private equity partner with a prediabetic A1C of 6.2. This guy was smart, successful, disciplined in business — but his health was falling apart.

[DIRECTION: Lean forward, more intense]

Rick had tried everything. Personal trainers who gave him 90-minute programs he couldn't stick to. Nutritionists who wanted him to meal prep on Sundays when he was closing deals. Apps that assumed he ate lunch at the same time every day.

But when we built him The Performance Stack — a system that accounted for his travel, his irregular schedule, his business dinners — everything clicked.

Six months later, his A1C dropped to 5.4 and he lost 28 pounds. His doctor couldn't believe the numbers.

[ON SCREEN: "Rick P. | A1C: 6.2 → 5.4 | Lost 28 lbs"]

But here's what really convinced me this was different...

Rick didn't just get results. He got results that lasted. Because the system was built around his reality, not some ideal version of his life.

[DIRECTION: Direct to camera]

And that's when I understood something that most trainers never figure out...

The executives who succeed long-term don't have more willpower than the ones who fail. They have better systems.

They understand that fitness isn't about finding time — it's about designing systems that work within the time you have.

They don't try to change their schedule to fit their workout. They build workouts that fit their schedule.

They don't meal prep for hours on Sunday. They have nutrition protocols that work whether they're eating at their desk or at a client dinner.

[ON SCREEN: "Systems > Willpower"]

That's The Performance Stack. And it's why executives are getting in the best shape of their lives without leaving their home office.

## 6. ACTION STEPS (12:00-15:00) [~450 words]

Alright, so how do you start building your own Performance Stack? Here are three steps you can take today:

[ON SCREEN: "The Performance Stack | 3 Action Steps"]

**Step 1: Audit Your Reality**

Stop planning your fitness around your ideal schedule and start planning around your actual schedule.

[DIRECTION: More tactical, direct]

Pull out your calendar from last month. Look at your actual meeting patterns, travel days, and work hours. When do you consistently have 20-30 minutes? Not when you wish you had time — when you actually have time.

Most executives discover they have three consistent windows: early morning before the chaos starts, lunch breaks that actually exist, or late evening after the kids are in bed.

Pick one. Just one. That's your training window.

**Step 2: Design for Disruption**

Your system has to work when everything goes wrong.

Build your nutrition protocol around business dinners, not meal prep. Build your training around hotel rooms, not home gyms. Build your recovery around travel schedules, not perfect sleep.

[ON SCREEN: "Design for disruption, not perfection"]

Here's a simple test: If your plan falls apart the moment you have a client emergency or need to travel, it's not a system — it's a wish list.

A real system gets stronger under pressure, just like your business does.

**Step 3: Track Leading Indicators, Not Lagging Indicators**

Stop obsessing over the scale and start tracking the behaviors that drive results.

[DIRECTION: Lean in, more emphasis]

Did you train in your scheduled window? Did you follow your nutrition protocol during your business dinner? Did you stick to your sleep routine while traveling?

These are leading indicators. They predict your results before the scale does.

The scale is a lagging indicator. It tells you what already happened, not what's about to happen.

[ON SCREEN: "Leading indicators predict results"]

Most executives fail because they optimize for lagging indicators. They get frustrated when the scale doesn't move after a week of good choices. But they ignore the leading indicators that guarantee long-term success.

Track the behaviors, and the results take care of themselves.

[DIRECTION: Wrap up, more encouraging]

Look, you already know how to build systems that scale. You do it in your business every day. The same principles that made you successful in business will make you successful in fitness.

You just need to stop trying to force fitness into someone else's system and start building a system around your reality.

That's The Performance Stack. That's how executives get in the best shape of their lives without leaving their home office.

## 7. CTA (15:00-End) [~300 words]

So remember — it's not about having more willpower or finding more time. It's about building systems that sync to your reality.

[DIRECTION: Paint the vision, more inspiring]

Imagine waking up tomorrow and feeling confident about the person looking back at you in that elevator mirror. Imagine having the energy to power through those 12-hour days and still show up for your kids. Imagine taking off your shirt at the beach without sucking in.

[ON SCREEN: "The Executive Athlete | Confident. Capable. Energetic."]

That's what happens when you become the executive athlete — a high performer who takes his body as seriously as his P&L.

Now, if you want to go deeper, I've created a free guide called "The Executive's 15-Minute Workout." It's designed specifically for executives who need results without leaving their home office.

[ON SCREEN: "Free Guide: The Executive's 15-Minute Workout"]

Click the link below to download it. No email required, no strings attached.

And if you're ready to build your complete Performance Stack with personalized coaching, applications for The Executive Athlete Program are open. I only work with 12 clients at a time because each executive gets a completely custom system.

[DIRECTION: Direct, no-nonsense]

But here's the thing — every month you wait is another month closer to the heart attack your father had at 58. Plus another $500 wasted on unused Peloton memberships and canceled trainers.

The question isn't whether you can afford to invest in your health. It's whether you can afford not to.

If this resonated with you, hit subscribe and ring the bell. I post tactical content for executives every week.

And drop a comment below — what's your biggest takeaway from this video?

Either way, thanks for watching. Now go build your system.

[ON SCREEN: "Subscribe | Apply for Coaching | Download Free Guide"]

---

# Instagram/Facebook Carousel Content for Marcus Reed

---

## Carousel #1 — Educational
**Carousel Title:** The 5-Step Travel Nutrition Protocol
**Core Belief Targeted:** Healthy eating is impossible with a travel schedule
**Funnel Stage:** TOF

### Slide 1
**Headline Text:** The 5-Step Travel Protocol
**Body Text:** How executives stay on track while traveling 2+ weeks per month. No meal prep required.
**Visual Direction:** Dark navy background, airplane icon, clean white text

### Slide 2
**Headline Text:** Step 1: Airport Strategy
**Body Text:** Skip the food court. Hit the newsstand for nuts, jerky, and protein bars. Plan your fuel before you board.
**Visual Direction:** Airport terminal background, shopping basket icon

### Slide 3
**Headline Text:** Step 2: Hotel Room Setup
**Body Text:** First stop isn't the bar. It's the nearest grocery store. Stock your mini-fridge like your kitchen.
**Visual Direction:** Hotel room visual, mini-fridge icon

### Slide 4
**Headline Text:** Step 3: Restaurant Rules
**Body Text:** Order first. Ask for protein + vegetables. Skip the bread basket and dessert menu entirely.
**Visual Direction:** Restaurant table setting, menu icon

### Slide 5
**Headline Text:** Step 4: Hydration Discipline
**Body Text:** One water bottle per hour of flight time. Dehydration kills performance and appetite control.
**Visual Direction:** Water bottle graphic, clock icon

### Slide 6
**Headline Text:** Step 5: Recovery Protocol
**Body Text:** Land, eat real food, sleep 8 hours. Your body doesn't care about time zones when it's properly fueled.
**Visual Direction:** Bed icon, recovery theme

### Slide 7
**Headline Text:** Want the Full System?
**Body Text:** Comment TRAVEL and I'll send you my complete executive travel nutrition guide.
**Visual Direction:** CTA design, message icon

**Caption:**
I've coached executives who travel 200+ days per year and still stay lean. The secret isn't willpower. It's having a system.

Most guys think business travel means fitness failure. Wrong. It means you need a different playbook.

These 5 steps kept Dan M. on track while traveling 2 weeks per month during his 42-pound transformation. Same system works whether you're in Dallas or Dubai.

Your schedule doesn't have to sabotage your health. You just need the right protocol.

Comment TRAVEL below and I'll DM you the complete guide.

**ManyChat Keyword:** TRAVEL
**What They Receive:** Complete executive travel nutrition guide

---

## Carousel #2 — Belief-Shifting
**Carousel Title:** Stop Chasing Perfect. Start Building Consistent.
**Core Belief Targeted:** I need the perfect program to see results
**Funnel Stage:** MOF

### Slide 1
**Headline Text:** Stop Chasing Perfect Programs
**Body Text:** Here's what actually moves the needle for busy executives.
**Visual Direction:** Bold red "STOP" sign, contrasting white text

### Slide 2
**Headline Text:** The Old Way: Perfect Plans
**Body Text:** Waiting for the ideal schedule. The perfect gym. The right meal prep Sunday. Meanwhile, another month passes.
**Visual Direction:** Calendar with X marks, frustrated executive silhouette

### Slide 3
**Headline Text:** Why It Fails
**Body Text:** Perfect doesn't exist in a 60-hour work week. You're optimizing for a life you don't actually live.
**Visual Direction:** Broken calendar, clock showing overtime hours

### Slide 4
**Headline Text:** The New Way: Flexible Systems
**Body Text:** Build around your reality. 15-minute workouts. Hotel gym protocols. Nutrition that travels with you.
**Visual Direction:** Flexible arrows, mobile phone showing workout

### Slide 5
**Headline Text:** Why It Works
**Body Text:** Consistency beats intensity. Rick P. lost 28 pounds with imperfect execution of a flexible system.
**Visual Direction:** Upward trending graph, success metrics

### Slide 6
**Headline Text:** Ready for Your System?
**Body Text:** Comment SYSTEM and I'll show you how to build fitness around your actual schedule.
**Visual Direction:** CTA design, gear/system icon

**Caption:**
The executives who transform aren't the ones with perfect schedules.

They're the ones who stopped waiting for perfect conditions.

I spent years watching guys delay their health because they didn't have the "right" setup. Perfect gym access. Perfect meal timing. Perfect sleep schedule.

Here's what I learned after coaching 340 clients: Perfect is the enemy of progress.

The Performance Stack isn't about perfect execution. It's about consistent execution within the reality of your life.

Stop optimizing for a schedule you don't have. Start building around the one you do.

Comment SYSTEM below and I'll show you exactly how.

**ManyChat Keyword:** SYSTEM
**What They Receive:** The Performance Stack overview guide

---

## Carousel #3 — Story
**Carousel Title:** From Burnt-Out Gym Owner to Online Executive Coach
**Core Belief Targeted:** Online coaching can't replace in-person training
**Funnel Stage:** TOF

### Slide 1
**Headline Text:** From 12-Hour Days to Freedom
**Body Text:** How I went from burnt-out gym owner to helping executives transform from anywhere.
**Visual Direction:** Split screen: exhausted vs. energized, transformation theme

### Slide 2
**Headline Text:** The Struggle Was Real
**Body Text:** 2021: Training 12 hours a day. Barely breaking even. Gaining weight while helping others lose it. Resenting every client.
**Visual Direction:** Dark, tired imagery, gym equipment background

### Slide 3
**Headline Text:** The Turning Point
**Body Text:** COVID hit. A client asked if I could train him over Zoom. I said yes. Then realized something game-changing.
**Visual Direction:** Lightbulb moment, video call interface

### Slide 4
**Headline Text:** The Realization
**Body Text:** I could help just as many people without a physical location. Better results. Better life. Better business.
**Visual Direction:** Home office setup, laptop showing workout

### Slide 5
**Headline Text:** The New Reality
**Body Text:** Closed the gym in 2022. Went fully online. Now I coach executives remotely with customized home programs.
**Visual Direction:** Success imagery, home gym setup, happy client

### Slide 6
**Headline Text:** Your Transformation Awaits
**Body Text:** Comment HOME and I'll send you my 15-minute executive workout guide.
**Visual Direction:** CTA design, home workout icon

**Caption:**
Sometimes the best business decision looks like the scariest one.

Closing my gym in 2022 felt like giving up. I'd invested everything into that location. Equipment. Lease. Staff. My identity was tied to that building.

But COVID taught me something: Results don't require a specific location. They require the right system and the right coach.

Now I help executives get in the best shape of their lives without leaving their home office. No commute. No excuses. No wasted time.

The transformation happens between your ears first. Then everything else follows.

Ready to prove that online coaching can deliver better results than anything you've tried?

Comment HOME below and I'll send you my 15-minute executive workout guide.

**ManyChat Keyword:** HOME
**What They Receive:** The Executive's 15-Minute Workout

---

## Carousel #4 — Results/Proof
**Carousel Title:** How Dan Lost 42 Pounds While Traveling 2 Weeks Per Month
**Core Belief Targeted:** I don't have time to train
**Funnel Stage:** BOF

### Slide 1
**Headline Text:** 42 Pounds in 6 Months
**Body Text:** How a SaaS CEO transformed while traveling 2 weeks per month. No gym membership required.
**Visual Direction:** Before/after transformation, business travel imagery

### Slide 2
**Headline Text:** The Before: Burnt Out
**Body Text:** Dan was 42 pounds over target. Hadn't trained in 3 years. Running a growing SaaS company on fumes.
**Visual Direction:** Stressed executive, airport scenes, tired businessman

### Slide 3
**Headline Text:** The System: Performance Stack
**Body Text:** Custom training synced to his travel schedule. Nutrition protocols for hotels and airports. Recovery strategies for jet lag.
**Visual Direction:** Three-layer system graphic, mobile app interface

### Slide 4
**Headline Text:** The Results: Transformed
**Body Text:** Lost 42 pounds. Squats 2x bodyweight. Travels 2 weeks per month and stays on track. Energy through the roof.
**Visual Direction:** Strong, confident executive, gym equipment, success metrics

### Slide 5
**Headline Text:** His Words, Not Mine
**Body Text:** "Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like." - Dan M., SaaS CEO
**Visual Direction:** Testimonial format, quote bubble, professional headshot placeholder

### Slide 6
**Headline Text:** Ready for Your Transformation?
**Body Text:** Comment RESULTS and I'll show you exactly how Dan did it.
**Visual Direction:** CTA design, transformation arrow

**Caption:**
"I don't have time" is the most expensive lie executives tell themselves.

Dan thought the same thing. SaaS CEO. Growing company. Travel 2 weeks per month. Zero time for conventional fitness.

But here's what he learned: You don't need more time. You need a better system.

The Performance Stack gave him exactly that. Training that fits in hotel rooms. Nutrition that works in airports. Recovery protocols for jet lag.

6 months later: 42 pounds down, stronger than ever, and more energy to run his business.

The time excuse disappeared when he found a system that worked with his reality, not against it.

Your schedule isn't the problem. Your approach is.

Comment RESULTS below and I'll show you exactly how Dan transformed while living on planes.

**ManyChat Keyword:** RESULTS
**What They Receive:** Dan's complete transformation case study

---

## Carousel #5 — Educational
**Carousel Title:** The 4-Step Hotel Gym Workout Protocol
**Core Belief Targeted:** Hotel gyms are useless for real training
**Funnel Stage:** TOF

### Slide 1
**Headline Text:** Hotel Gym Workout Protocol
**Body Text:** How to get a killer workout with dumbbells, a bench, and 30 minutes. No excuses.
**Visual Direction:** Hotel gym setting, professional workout theme

### Slide 2
**Headline Text:** Step 1: Movement Assessment
**Body Text:** 5 minutes of dynamic warm-up. Check what equipment actually works. Plan your circuit before you start.
**Visual Direction:** Stretching figure, equipment check icons

### Slide 3
**Headline Text:** Step 2: Compound Focus
**Body Text:** Dumbbell squats, chest press, rows. Hit multiple muscles per exercise. Maximum efficiency.
**Visual Direction:** Compound exercise illustrations, efficiency arrows

### Slide 4
**Headline Text:** Step 3: Circuit Structure
**Body Text:** 3 exercises, 45 seconds each, 15 seconds rest. Repeat 4 rounds. Heart rate up, strength challenged.
**Visual Direction:** Timer graphic, circuit flow diagram

### Slide 5
**Headline Text:** Step 4: Finish Strong
**Body Text:** 5-minute core circuit. Plank variations. Mountain climbers. Leave nothing in the tank.
**Visual Direction:** Core exercise icons, completion checkmark

### Slide 6
**Headline Text:** Get the Full Protocol
**Body Text:** Comment HOTEL and I'll send you the complete hotel gym workout guide.
**Visual Direction:** CTA design, hotel key card icon

**Caption:**
Hotel gyms get a bad rap. Usually deserved.

But I've helped executives build serious strength with nothing but hotel dumbbells and a bench.

The secret isn't the equipment. It's the protocol.

Most guys walk into a hotel gym and wing it. 20 minutes of random exercises. No plan. No intensity. No results.

Here's what works: Compound movements. Circuit structure. Time efficiency. Maximum muscle recruitment in minimum time.

This 4-step protocol has kept my traveling executives on track in hotels from Austin to Amsterdam.

Your location doesn't determine your results. Your system does.

Comment HOTEL below and I'll send you the complete protocol.

**ManyChat Keyword:** HOTEL
**What They Receive:** Complete hotel gym workout guide

---

## Carousel #6 — Belief-Shifting
**Carousel Title:** Stop Waiting for Monday. Start Today.
**Core Belief Targeted:** I'll start after this quarter ends
**Funnel Stage:** BOF

### Slide 1
**Headline Text:** Stop Waiting for Monday
**Body Text:** Why "I'll start next quarter" is costing you years of progress.
**Visual Direction:** Calendar pages flying, urgency theme

### Slide 2
**Headline Text:** The Delay Trap
**Body Text:** After this project. After this quarter. After this busy season. There's always another excuse waiting.
**Visual Direction:** Endless calendar, procrastination imagery

### Slide 3
**Headline Text:** The Real Cost
**Body Text:** Every month of delay is another month closer to the heart attack your father had at 58. Time doesn't wait.
**Visual Direction:** Clock ticking, health warning imagery

### Slide 4
**Headline Text:** The Truth About Timing
**Body Text:** Perfect timing doesn't exist. The best time was 5 years ago. The second best time is right now.
**Visual Direction:** Timeline graphic, "NOW" emphasis

### Slide 5
**Headline Text:** Start Where You Are
**Body Text:** 15 minutes, 3 times per week. Build the habit first. Scale the intensity later. Progress beats perfection.
**Visual Direction:** Small steps leading upward, momentum arrows

### Slide 6
**Headline Text:** Ready to Start Today?
**Body Text:** Comment START and I'll send you the 15-minute workout to begin right now.
**Visual Direction:** CTA design, play button icon

**Caption:**
"I'll start after this quarter ends."

I've heard this from every executive I've ever coached. Including the ones who waited 3 years to finally begin.

Here's what I learned: There will always be another quarter. Another project. Another busy season.

The executives who transform are the ones who start in the middle of chaos. Not after it.

Rick P. started his transformation during his company's biggest acquisition. Lost 28 pounds while closing the deal.

Dan M. began training during a product launch. Lost 42 pounds while scaling his SaaS company.

Your schedule will never be perfect. Your health can't wait for it to be.

The best workout program is the one you start today. Not the perfect one you'll start someday.

Comment START below and I'll send you the 15-minute workout to begin right now.

**ManyChat Keyword:** START
**What They Receive:** The Executive's 15-Minute Workout
